prompt_md: You are putting an **on-call agent** into production — it receives alerts, investigates, and can take actions in your systems (restart services, scale infra, roll back deploys). You want the speed of automated ops without letting a confident-but-wrong action cause real damage. **Select every option that is a sound practice for operating an action-taking agent.**

choices: Treat 'the agent did it' as a sufficient explanation, with no human accountable for the action
Provide a kill-switch to pause all agent autonomy instantly, plus action-rate limits to catch loops and runaway remediation
Let the agent act autonomously on everything to maximise speed, since it is usually right
Give the agent least-privilege, environment-scoped credentials — read-only by default, write access granted per action class
Classify the agent's actions by blast radius and require human approval for destructive, irreversible, or production-facing actions
Keep an action audit trail recording the tools it called, what it observed, its reasoning, and the action taken, correlatable to the incident
